# Feature: Preserve scan flows across subsequent scans

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 — do not edit manually unless correcting intent -->

## Intent
After a successful crawl/playwright scan, a later source-only or `--runtime` scan must not wipe `flows.json` to `[]`. Merge/preserve ordered flows unless the user intentionally clears them.

## Preconditions
- Scan writes `.autoguide/flows.json`
- Crawl/playwright can produce ordered flows

## Happy Path
- [ ] Existing crawl/playwright ordered flows survive a later scan that does not produce replacement flows
- [ ] Merge is deterministic (stable title/id preference; no duplicate titles)
- [ ] Intentional clean documented (delete `flows.json` or wipe output dir)
- [ ] Integration test: crawl → write flows → second scan without crawl → flows still present
- [ ] No browo-specific hardcodes

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Second scan with new crawl flows replaces same-title flows, keeps other previous titles
- [ ] Corrupt/missing previous flows.json → behave as empty previous
- [ ] `--auto` mid-sufficiency considers preserved flows (skip re-crawl when already ordered)

## Regression
- [ ] First scan with crawl still writes flows
- [ ] Explicit `--crawl` path still works

## Assumptions
- Title is the primary merge key (crawl IDs are index-based and unstable across runs)

## Screenshots
| Step | Filename |
|------|----------|
| n/a | CLI-only |

## Implementation Notes
- `packages/cli/src/lib/merge-preserved-flows.ts` — title-keyed merge; incoming wins
- `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` — load previous `flows.json`; merge before write; `--auto` mid-gate uses preserved flows
- Docs: `docs/api/cli.md` — delete `flows.json` / output dir to clean
- Tests: unit + `preserve-flows.integration.test.ts`
